entry.title: File-based journal entry imports
entry.content: The “Post Tax Payables” app does not currently support file-based journal entry imports. To improve operational efficiency, we request this function to be added.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud：增强版 New User Experience Switch 全面解析
entry.content:
在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中，SAP 持续推进用户体验（UX）的革新，其中 New User Experience Switch（新版用户体验切换功能） 是帮助企业顺利完成应用替换的重要机制。最新版本（2602）对该功能进行了进一步增强，让业务用户与管理员更容易适应新旧应用的切换进程。 

         本文将带你了解这个增强功能的核心设计、工作方式，以及企业在实际使用中可以获得的价值。

一、为什么需要 New User Experience Switch？

         SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 每 6 个月发布一次新版本，其中会逐步引入新应用（phase-in），同时也可能逐步淘汰旧应用（phase-out）。通常，业务用户有 至少 12 个月 的窗口期来适应新的替代应用，但在实际项目中，这段时间可能转瞬即逝。 

         为避免用户错过迁移时机，SAP 引入 New User Experience Switch，通过界面提示与切换选项来“温和推动”用户尝试并过渡到新应用。

二、New User Experience Switch 的三种提示阶段

         切换功能在应用生命周期的不同阶段会呈现不同的提示样式：

1. Information 信息阶段

  • 告诉用户有新的应用版本正在被引入
  • 展示新应用的优势
  • 鼓励用户尝试
  • 用户可以随时切回旧应用

         这是“最温和”的阶段，旨在减少用户对改变的抵触情绪。

2. Warning 警告阶段

         当旧应用已经被标记为 deprecated（即将淘汰） 时，切换功能会显示黄色的警告信息：

  • 告知旧应用将在未来版本中不再提供
  • 提醒用户尽快完成迁移

3. Alert 紧急阶段

         这是旧应用被移除前的最后一个版本，切换功能会以更醒目的方式提示：

  • 强调迁移的紧迫性
  • 显示明确的移除时间点

         在这个阶段，如果用户仍然停留在旧应用上，会面临无法继续使用的风险。

三、切换功能如何呈现？

         SAP 在 2502 版本引入 New User Experience Switch，在 2508 与 2602 的版本中进一步增强：

  • 旧应用启动时出现提示 popover（气泡提示）
  • Shell Bar（顶端工具栏）中出现 切换按钮
  • 用户可在新旧应用之间自由切换（取决于阶段）

         这些交互方式让用户无需离开当前体验即可了解版本状态并快速做出选择。

四、对业务用户意味着什么？

✔ 更轻松的学习曲线

不再需要由管理员单独告知新旧应用替代关系，系统会自动提示。

✔ 提前感知变化

避免新版本发布后突然发现旧应用“消失”，有效减少工作中断。

✔ 自主试用新应用

在 phase-in 阶段，用户可以自由在新旧体验中切换，加速熟悉度提升。

五、对管理员意味着什么？

管理员可以利用此功能：

  • 提前规划应用替换节奏
  • 知道哪些用户仍在使用旧应用
  • 在企业层面推动统一迁移

这让应用治理更加可控，也能减少升级周期带来的沟通成本。

六、为什么说这个功能“让改变更容易”？

New User Experience Switch 正是帮助企业完成改变的机制：

  • 让改变更加可见
  • 让迁移更加自主
  • 让用户更有掌控感

对于每一个正在推进数字化、云化的企业来说，这种设计都能有效降低内部阻力。

结语：更智慧、更温和的用户体验演进机制

         New User Experience Switch 的增强不仅让用户更轻松地迈向 SAP Fiori 的现代化体验，也让管理员和企业能够更从容、可控地管理应用生命周期。

         随着 SAP 持续对 UX 的改进（例如 2602 版本中的 AI 辅助、增强的 Launchpad、手机端优化等），未来企业的 SAP 使用体验将更加智能、一致、顺滑。 

         如果你正在规划 S/4HANA Cloud 的上云旅程或准备迎接新版本，这项功能值得你重点关注。

 

参考文档：SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud – New User Experie... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Manage Product master data / work scheduling view / p...
entry.content:
Good day @Florian83 

Thank you for your question.

The Work Scheduling view is specific to plants, and it only persists once you have maintained the required plant data in the product master. So, the recommended best practice is to create the product with the correct views up front (or you may also copy from an existing template product), then create BOM and routing and finally create the production version.

See the following references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/0483875792f25c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3c916ef10fc240c9afc594b346ffaf77/bbf21592fac54989aaba227d684e633d.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/5fbc1f91f9b246dd9208245f96ba7021.html?locale=en-US

The best practice is to sequence:

Step 1: To use the Manage Product Master Data app,

Create and enter your product basic data.

Include Basic Data, Plant Data for MRP, Work Scheduling and any other views you need.

 

Step 2 and 3: Then in your Maintain Bill of Material App, create or assign the BOM for the product.


 

Step 4: In your Manage Production Versions app, create production by assigning BOM, routing or recipe reference, validity dates and lot size range.

You can avoid that by:

Option A is copying from a template product (Best practice).  Use Manage Product Master Data and copy from a correctly maintained reference product. This brings required plant views and default values so Work Scheduling is already consistent and you only adjust what is different.

Option B is to ensure mandatory defaults are set by configuration. If your customers create products repeatedly without knowing what to fill in Work Scheduling, then it’s best to set defaults where permitted so Work Scheduling fields are prefilled and the first save persists the view cleanly.

You may also consider using migration (Migrate your Data App) for mass creation for larger volumes as option C to load products and production versions in a managed and repeatable.

See the following reference for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/cdccca8e03d74101a0135863bc522b49/d93d815401ad4f6ba698c97a2f70999c.html?locale=en-US

https://www.sap.com/documents/2020/07/7a263781-a67d-0010-87a3-c30de2ffd8ff.html

To summarise:

  • Work Scheduling is not truly created for the plant until you maintain required fields.
  • Best sequence is Product (with Work Scheduling) --> BOM -->  Routing -->  Production Version.
  • Simplify with Copy from template product or use Migration Cockpit if you have volumes to manage.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Service Definition based on Consumption CDS View (BOPF-Based CDS View)
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I am curious if it is possible to create a Service Definition based on the BOPF-based consumption CDS View? This would be theoretically an easier way to create and publish OData V2 or V4 services in SAP, but still not replacing all the backbone from the old programming mode for SAP Fiori to ABAP Restful Programming Model (RAP).

I tried this on an SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 system and the service definition and service binding could be created and activated without any problems, but later one when checking the metadata of the new OData V4 service I am receiving some strange issues regarding a quantity field and its unit of measure

Unit code property 'DAIRYDELIVPROPOSALBASEQTY' not found in complex type 'CT_B93A2928B399FBCED4EC7994740'

it is quite strange since for me the related annotations are properly defined in the consumption CDS View.

Is anyone tried this out and maybe could confirm if this is working or not?

Many thanks,
Catalin

entry.title: Re: change selection screen variable for KE30 report. ECC
entry.content:
Steps to Add a Variable for KE30

1) Change Report
Go to GR22 (Change Report) or GRR2 (Change Report Group). Identify the report or report group that KE30 is executing.

2) Define the Variable in the Library
Transaction: GRV1 (Create Variable) or GRV2 (Change Variable). Select the library used by your KE30 report (e.g., 1VK, 8A, etc.). Create a new variable for the characteristic (e.g., Company Code, Profit Center, Fiscal Year). Specify the variable type (single value, interval, set, etc.) and whether it should appear on the selection screen.

3) Assign the Variable to the Report
In GR22, edit the report layout. For the row/column where you want dynamic input, assign the variable instead of a fixed value. Save the report.

4) Execution in KE30
When you run KE30, the system will now prompt for the variable on the selection screen. The variable values entered by the user will drive the report output.

Important Notes

  • Variables are library-specific. If your KE30 report uses multiple libraries, ensure the variable is created in the correct one.
  • You can control whether the variable is mandatory, optional, or defaulted.
  • Always transport the variable definition and report changes through your landscape (DEV → QA → PROD).

entry.title: WB2R111 error
entry.content:
Hello!

I'm running WB2R_SC, our aim is to make the final settlement with the date 31.12.2025

but  WB2R111 - "not all partial settlements could be distributed" error thrown.

Based on the line I’ve marked on the attached image, I’m wondering if the WB2R111 error might be caused by a manual settlement that was create on June.

I’ve noticed that other condition contracts which are currently experiencing the same error also contain manual entries. Could it be that these manual settlements are interfering with the system’s ability to correctly match business volume lines during final settlement?

I’ve checked and found the most suitable OSS note for this error - 3405475 

And I put breakpoint  in the method, mentioned in the OSS note and made debugging  while running  WB2R_SC transaction.  I see system found 1714 lines for partial settlement , however ALL  of their PRCTR values are empty.

by the way, we are using custom Bus. volume table ZSD_VBRKVBRP_6 with split criteria on Z field that's appended on structure KOMLFP. 

Based on the information I’ve provided, how can we run the report successfully and bypass the error?

thans in advance!

entry.title: Re: Error in vk11
entry.content:
The error message “Enter a valid value” in VK11 usually isn’t about the material itself being “wrong,” but rather about the condition record input not matching what the system expects. Here are the most common causes:

Why the Error Appears

Incorrect Key Combination:
Each condition type (e.g., PR00 for price, K004 for material discount) has a defined access sequence. If you’re entering a material where the condition type doesn’t expect it, the system rejects it.

Material Master Issues:
Material may not exist in the sales organization/distribution channel you’re working in. Material might be flagged for deletion or not extended to the relevant sales area.

Organizational Data Mismatch:
Wrong sales org, distribution channel, or division entered. Material not assigned to the plant or sales org you’re using.

Condition Table Setup:
If the condition table for that condition type doesn’t include “Material” as a key field, you can’t enter it in VK11.

Formatting/Validity:
Sometimes the error is triggered by typos, leading zeros, or entering a material number without the correct format (e.g., missing leading zeros if the material number is numeric).

How to Troubleshoot

Check Material Master (MM03)
Verify the material exists and is extended to the correct sales org/distribution channel. Confirm it’s not flagged for deletion.

Check Condition Type (V/06)
Look at the access sequence and condition table. Confirm “Material” is a valid key field for that condition type.

Check Organizational Data in VK11
Make sure you’re entering the right sales org/distribution channel/division combination.

Try Another Material
If another material works, the issue is likely with the master data setup of the original material.

entry.title: Re: Receive Events from Event Mesh of Integration Suite to CAP Node.js Applications
entry.content:
Hi Andrea, good question! Based on your scenario, I would recommend upgrading to Advanced Event Mesh - it's designed exactly for situations like yours. 

Advanced Event Mesh offers different service classes with higher connection limits. For 400 connections, you'd simply select the Broker 1K class, which supports up to 1,000 concurrent client connections. 

Unlike the standalone Event Mesh with its 200-connection hard limit, a single Advanced Event Mesh instance with Broker 1K handles all 400 connections. Your CAP app and all iFlows can connect to one broker, simplifying your architecture significantly.

entry.title: Re: Business has a custom line item numbering for Sales D...
entry.content:
Hello @bigflatfoot 

First, challenge the requirement. There is all but zero business value in it. It is not SAP standard. It can't be implemented within clean core. The requirement should have been rejected all together.

Second, explain that the custom item numbering can't be implemented when items are created in VA01 or VA02. If it can be implemented at all, the items need to be renumbered when a sales order is saved.

Third, you can try to renumber the items with USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE enhancement, include MV45AFZZ. I'm not sure if it's going to work, but it's the only way I can see.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to make a one-time annual accrual on top of the d...
entry.content:
Hi @MarioneDelaCruz ,

If you normally accrue 0.05 days per day and want to add an extra 1 day every October 1st, you can set up your accrual rule like this:

If Today = October 1st → Post 1.05 days

Else→ Post 0.05 days

you can use Createdate() to compare against October1st.

Regards,

VR

entry.title: Setup Actions and Events for integration in SAP Build Process Automation for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
This blog explains how to do the setup for Actions for a Sales Order scenario and how to consume this in SAP Build Process Automation. It also has reference for business events setup.

 

Event Setup

If you are using event-based integration for starting the process using Event Triggers please refer to the links for the setup S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition ( Configure SAP Enterprise Messaging for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise 2020 via Service Key ) and S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition ( Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup for SAP Build Process Automation).  Here is a detailed link describing how to use Event Triggers in SAP Build Process Automation Discovering Business Events from Lobby for SAP Build Process Automation : Manage Credit Memo Request

 

Actions Project Setup

“Actions” in SAP Build Process Automation connects processes to external systems, whether SAP or non-SAP. This is crucial for automating or expanding business processes for various LoB processes such as S/4HANA, Ariba, and SuccessFactors. With SAP Build Process Automation, these extensions can be easily built, and through actions, connections can be made to S/4HANA, Ariba, or other SAP LoB systems for GET, POST, PATCH, and other types of calls.

In this blog we will create an action project based on Sales Order API with the following steps.

  • Step 1 - Set Up Connection to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition
  • Step 2 - Set up Communication Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Edition
  • Step 3 - Add destination in SAP Build
  • Step 4 - Create Action Project in SAP Build
  • Step 5 - Test the Action Project
  • Step 6 - Release and Publish the Action Project.

Step 1 – Set Up Connection to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition

1)  Enable APIs in SAP Gateway Client

Please follow this blog post to enable the API for the service API_SALES_ORDER_SRV.

2)    Configure Cloud Connector

Please follow this blog post steps to configure the cloud connector.

  • Configure Destination for SAP Build Process Automation
  • After the Cloud Connector is configured, you should be able to see the Cloud Connector in your SAP BTP Subaccount under Connectivity -> Cloud Connectors.
  • Now let’s create destination for Sales Order API from BTP Cockpit->  Subaccount -> Destinations

  • Make sure to provide the following additional properties while creating the destination:

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true

sap.processautomation.enabled = true

sap-client=<ClientID>

  • Click on Check Connection and test that the connection is successful.

Step 2: Set up Communication Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

Prerequisite: please make sure you should have an administrator access for SAP S/4HANA system.

For the following setup, we will use communication scenario for Sales Order Integration (SAP_COM_0109).

Optional: You can learn more about communication scenarios here.

  1. Create Communication User

1.1 Login to SAP S/4HANA Cloud System and select 'Communication Management' group:

1.2 Select ‘Maintain Communication Users’ App and click 'New':

1.3 Afterwards, go ahead and provide User Name, Description & Password and click 'Create':

1.4 Once you click 'Create', the Password Status becomes Productive. Now click 'Save': 

Great! The Communication User is now successfully created.

Next up, Communication System and Communication Arrangement need to be created.

2. Create Communication System:

2.1 Login to SAP S/4HANA Cloud System, select 'Communication Management' group and Click 'Communication Systems' app (similar to step 1.1).

2.2 Here you would see the list of already existing Communication Systems if any. Click 'New' to create a new Communication System:

2.3 Enter a System ID and System Name and click 'Create':

2.4 Provide the Host Name and HTTPS Port of the system you want to connect to.

We will use a hypothetical name and port here.

Click 'Add' button for inbound communication to add the user for authenticating inbound request:

Note: Here we are using inbound service hence the 'Logical System' is optional but when we make use of outbound service then 'Logical System' plays an important role and is mandatory to be filled.

2.5 Select the Communication User (from value help) which we had just created. The assigned Communication User will now automatically appear in Username field. Click 'Save'.

2.6 Click 'Save' and the Communication System is created:

Now we have successfully created a Communication System with a Communication User assigned to it.

3. Create Communication arrangement based on the relevant Communication Scenario:

3.1 Login to SAP S/4HANA Cloud System, select 'Communication Management' group and Click ‘Communication Arrangement’ app (like step 2.1).

3.2 Once you are within the 'Communication Arrangements' application you would see the existing Communication Arrangements if any. Click 'New':

3.3 Here select the relevant 'Communication Scenario' (from value help) based on the API you would want to access. Like in our example we will use Sales Order Integration (SAP_COM_0109) for this case and click 'Create':

 

Note: You would be able find relevant Communication Scenarios in the API documentation from SAP API Business Hub.

3.4 Select the 'Communication System' (from value help) which we have just created. The assigned Communication User will now automatically appear in User Name field. Click 'Save':

The Communication Arrangement is now created successfully.

4. Configure Destination for SAP Build Process Automation

We will now create the destination for Sales Order APIs. 

First, go to SAP BTP Cockpit -> Subaccount -> Destinations. You will see the available destination listed. Make sure that the additional properties are as below:

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true

sap.processautomation.enabled = true

sap-client=<ClientID>

In this example, we are using the destination DCOM_Sales_Order as a reference only.

And finally, click on Check Connection to test it.

The connection should indicate OK:

Step 3: Add destination in SAP Build

Next, to use this destination, we will connect it to SAP Build Process Automation. From the SAP Build lobby, click on Control Tower.

Click on Destinations, to configure the destination with SAP Build:

Look for the Destination name from the previous step in the search bar and click on the 3 dots: 

Afterwards, we specify the Environment in which the destination will be configured to. Choose any environment  (here the screenshot shows Public) and Add Destination.

Environments refer to the different stages or platforms where the automated processes will run, such as development, testing, and production. Each environment may have different sets of configurations and requirements.

 

 

Step 4: Create Action Project in SAP Build

  1. Please download the pre-configured Action Project by clicking here. You will download the mtar. file from GitHub to your device as below:

2. Now head on over to the SAP Build Lobby. Click on the Import function:

3. Browse the imported file from your machine to import it. 

 

4. Great, now that you have imported the Action project. Let's review it. Go to Actions function on the left-hand side. Then, double click on the Sales Order project.

5. This will open the Actions Editor function in a new tab with the Action project downloaded. As you can see there are 3 APIs inside:

Step 5 - Test the Action Project

It is now time to test the APIs. Let's start testing starting with the GET API: Reads the header of a sales order. Click on it:

 

After clicking on the configured destination, click on the Test tab and provide a valid SalesOrder number. Click on the Test button:

 

Test response reading the header of the relevant Sales Order will be provided as the following:

 

Now, from the Actions tab, and let's test the other GET API: Reads all items of a Sales Order by following the same steps as before:

 

Check Response:

 

Great job! We have now configured and tested the actions you downloaded successfully for our exercise.

Step 6 - Release and Publish the Action Project

 

You will now release the action project to create version(s) and then publish that version in the action repository. It is then these published actions that can be used in different processes and applications to connect to external systems.

To release a version of the action project, click Release from top-right corner.

 

In the release popup, enter the Release Notes of your choice and click Release.

If you have Publish to Library button instead of Release, please directly Publish as prescribed in the next step.

 

Once the action project is released, you can then publish the action by clicking Publish from top-right corner and confirm Publish. Wait for confirmation that it is published.

 

With this you have successfully completed creating, configuring, releasing and publishing of action project.

Now you can use these published actions in any SAP Build Process Automation project.

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP Platform Automation #2 (March Edition)
entry.content:
📣The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open!

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for btp CLI or Terraform on SAP BTP send me an email and we will get back to you.

Introduction

In this monthly series we show and discuss all things around automating SAP BTP with the btp CLI or the Terraform providers from SAP.

📼You find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous roundtables in this on this GitHub page

Meeting Information

When?

  • March 24th, 11:00 – 12:00 CET – Teams Webinar Registration (please register in advance)

Who?

  • All interested customer, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable

What’s up (aka preliminary agenda)?

    • Challenge: credential rotation via Terrform
  • UX Survey for btp CLI and Terraform

  • Q + A

✨Looking forward to meeting you!

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ Set Product Price dynamically from attribute...
entry.content:
Hi @isidora_solvetect, I applied this formula in the change trigger attribute, and it works:

attr = Product.Attributes.GetBySystemId("ATTR_Certificazioni_analisi_cpq")
value = attr.GetValue()

Trace.Write("Attr " + attr.Name + " - Value: " + str(value))

if value is not None:
    for v in attr.Values:
        v.PriceFormula = "<*Value(Certificazioni analisi)*>"

 Thanks

entry.title: Re: Edit Date via Date Action
entry.content: Hi @spurwar, thanks for the help. In the end, I managed to do it too with a similar solution. Here's what I did: INTEGER @DAYS FLOAT @AMOUNT_VALUE FOREACH [d/Data_Fattura], [d/Company], [d/Liquidity_Item] @DAYS = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Payment_day") @AMOUNT_VALUE = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Amount") IF @DAYS > 0 THEN DATA([d/Data_Pianificazione] = NEXT(@DAYS, "DAY", [d/Data_Fattura]), [d/Liquidity_Item] = [d/Liquidity_Item],[d/Measures] = "Payment_day") = @DAYS DATA([d/Data_Pianificazione] = NEXT(@DAYS, "DAY", [d/Data_Fattura]), [d/Liquidity_Item] = [d/Liquidity_Item],[d/Measures] = "Amount") = @AMOUNT_VALUE ENDIF ENDFOR

entry.title: Re: Is Actual Costing (Scope Item 33Q) feasible in SAP S/...
entry.content:
Hello @hajersoula 

Thank you for your question.

Question: Can Actual Costing (Scope Item 33Q) still be activated in a scenario where materials are set to Price Control V and others to Price Control S?

Answer: Yes, you can activate and use Actual Costing even if some materials are Price Control V, but those V materials do not participate in actual costing revaluation in the same way. In practice, when you consider the Ledger logic, Actual Costing is meant for materials that are valuated at Standard Pricing during the period and then revaluated at the end of the period. Materials that remain Moving Average are valuated with moving average logic and are not treated the same way in actual costing.

So, the key point here is:

  • It is mandatory to change every single material in the client to S just to activate 33Q
  • It is mandatory that the materials you want to be included in actual costing revaluation follow the required price determination and standard price logic.

Also note that there are valid business cases where you cannot switch material to S. An example is, certain batch valuation designs can enforce V. See KBA 3417714 for more details.

For your step, I recommend:

  • Keeping RM as V if the business intentionally wants moving average.
  • Ensuring SFG and FG that you want in actual costing are consistently set up to participate.
  • Avoid having the same material category randomly split between S and V unless you have a clear policy.

 

Question: Is it mandatory in Public Cloud to set all materials to Standard Price (S) to use Actual Costing?

Answer: Referencing the earlier key point, the answer is NO.  

 

Question: If Actual Costing cannot be used due to the presence of materials under Price Control V, are there any alternative solutions or processes available in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to calculate or approximate the actual cost

Answer: For materials with Price Control V, the system already updates valuation based on actual postings (moving average logic). If you need to adjust the periodic unit price for V materials, I suggest you use the supported approach via Change Material Costs and not changing the master directly.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/bbd9b6ce0be14516ad7868da0982024a.html?locale=en-US

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2931804

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Default view of choose template tab during document g...
entry.content:
1. Create a new quote or load existing quote.
  2. Click on Generate document.
  3. Reach on 'Choose Template' section.
  4. Templates are listed (default view).
  5. To switch to the grid view by click the option located next to the template search icon at the top of the screen. 
  6. There is a button to change the view to list or tabular(grid) view.
  7. When you select either view, the setting is remembered whilst you a logged in an applied to all quotes that you are generating documents for.
  8. One benefit of the list view is that it allows you to view the template description by hovering over the template name.

entry.title: Re: Discovery Package: Test, Demo, and Development for SAP Cloud ERP Private
entry.content:
@dnsteiner 

hi,

The fully activated appliance available through CAL is an on‑premise system. Because of this architecture, connecting it to BTP—whether for using Joule or for future cloud‑based innovations—is not supported and is technically not possible.
Functionally, the CAL system contains essentially the same content as the Discovery Package: the same clients, the same best practices in the same activation state, and the same scenarios including demo guides. Only the new features introduced in the respective releases differ.
However, the service models are fundamentally different. In CAL, customers deploy and operate the on‑premise image themselves. In contrast, the Discovery Package runs entirely within an SAP‑owned environment, and the corresponding SLAs are designed in a way that keeps overall costs as low as possible.

best, Bettina

entry.title: はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - デザインのTips
entry.content:
本ブログシリーズはSAP Analytics CloudのBI機能を使ってダッシュボードやレポートの作成を行う方を対象とし、SAP Analytics CloudのBIにおける基本の操作やTipsをハンズオン形式でご紹介します。

本ブログでは、ストーリー作成時に役立つデザインのTipsをいくつかご紹介します。

 

目次：

  • ロゴ画像を挿入してみよう！
  • 背景色を変えてみよう！
  • 複数のウィジェットを一つのパネルにまとめてみよう！
  • ウィジェットの位置や大きさを細かく調整しよう！（キャンバスレイアウト）
  • チャートを綺麗に整列させよう！
  • オリジナルのカラーパレットを作成しよう！
  • （参考）完成したストーリーをテンプレートとして保存しよう！

※ SAP Analytics Cloudは、四半期に１度のペースで製品のバージョンアップが実施されます。そのため今後のバージョンアップにより、本ブログで紹介する画面キャプチャや、操作が異なる可能性があることをご了承ください。（本ブログは2026年3月に作成しています。）

 

ロゴ画像を挿入してみよう！

ストーリーに画像を挿入することができます。会社のロゴなどを入れてみましょう！

1. 挿入 > ＋ > その他 > 画像をクリックします。

2. 「＋画像の追加」をクリックし、任意の画像を選択します。

画像を挿入することができました！

 

背景色を変えてみよう！

ストーリーの背景の色を好きな色に変更してみましょう！

1. 右パネル（スタイルパネル）を開き、ストーリーの余白をクリックします。キャンバス > 背景色の「ｖ」をクリックします。

2. 好きな色を選び、「OK」をクリックします。

3. （オプション）各ウィジェットの背景色を変更します。（デフォルトでは色なしのため背景と同化してしまいます）
ウィジェットをクリックし（Shift＋クリックで複数選択でもOK）、ウィジェット > 背景色の「ｖ」をクリックして色を変更します。

背景色を変更することができました！


複数のウィジェットを一つのパネルにまとめてみよう！

パネルを挿入して複数のウィジェットをまとめてスッキリさせてみましょう！

1. 挿入 > ＋ > コンテナ > パネルをクリックします。
2. 任意のウィジェットを挿入されたパネルにドラッグ＆ドロップします。

パネルの背景色を変えて位置を調整することで、複数のウィジェットを一つのエリアにまとめることができました！

 

ウィジェットの位置や大きさを細かく調整しよう！（キャンバスレイアウト）

キャンバスレイアウトでストーリーを作成する際、マウスを使ってウィジェットの角で調整するだけでなく、ウィジェットの位置や大きさをピクセル単位で細かく調整することができます。

1. 右パネル（スタイルパネル）を開き、任意のウィジェットをクリックします。サイズと位置でピクセル単位で変更することができます。

 


2. （オプション）ピクセル単位ではなく、パーセントで設定することも可能です。パーセントで設定すると、ストーリーを開く画面の画素数が変更されてもウィジェットがそれに伴って大きさが変わり、画面いっぱいに表示することができておすすめです。（全てのオブジェクトをパーセントで設定する必要があります）

イメージ：

 3. （オプション）ウィジェットの角を丸くすることができます。スタイルパネルのウィジェット > 枠線を「すべての枠線」に変更し、コーナー半径を任意の値に変更します。

チャートを綺麗に整列させよう！

散らばっている複数のウィジェットを一気に整列させてみましょう！

1. ウィジェットをShift＋クリックで複数選択肢、「•••」をクリックし、ウジェットを整列より任意の操作を選択します。
ウィジェットが綺麗に整列しました！
 

オリジナルのカラーパレットを作成しよう！

チャートの色を好きな色で表示するオリジナルのカラーパレットを活用してみましょう！企業のイメージカラーに統一することでよりオリジナリティのあるストーリーを作成することができます。

1. 右パネル（ビルダパネル）を開き、チャートをクリックします。色内のデフォルトで設定されているパレットの鉛筆マークをクリックします。

2. ストーリーパレットの「＋」をクリックします

3. 任意の色を設定し、「保存」をクリックします。 
オリジナルのカラーパレットで表示できました！

（参考）完成したストーリーをテンプレートとして保存しよう！

綺麗に整えたストーリーをベースとして他のストーリーを作成できるようにしたい場合、テンプレートとして保存しておくことで効率的にストーリーを作成することができます。

1. ファイル > 保存 > テンプレートとして保存... をクリックします。


2.  任意の名前を入力して「保存」をクリックします。

3. 保存したテンプレートからストーリーを作成してみます。ナビゲーションバー > ストーリー > テンプレート より保存したテンプレートをクリックします。

テンプレートを活用してストーリーの作成ができるようになりました！

 

今回はデザインのTipsの紹介でした。SAP Analytics Cloudは他にも様々な表現や便利な機能があり、本ブログシリーズでご紹介しています。
はじめてのSAP Analytics Cloud BI - シリーズまとめ - SAP Communityからご参照ください。

entry.title: New System Based Certification for SAP S/4HANA Asset Management Available!
entry.content:
Starting in 2026, SAP is introducing brand‑new system‑based certifications.
These updated exams consist of real, hands‑on tasks executed directly in an SAP system. The result: a much stronger focus on applied skills and real‑world capability. In the area of Asset Management, we developed a certification for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Asset Management

How to Prepare Successfully

  1. Explore the official Learning Journey (free on learning.sap.com)

👉 Implementing Asset Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

  1. Get hands‑on practice

Use the available hands‑on training systems to run through exercises, demos, and end‑to‑end scenarios.
This practical experience is key for the new system‑based exam format. Hands-on systems can be found in the Learning Journey.

  1. Join the “Get Certified!” Live Sessions

SAP experts provide guidance, highlight important focus areas, and answer your questions — perfect for a confident exam day. Join the sessions or watch the recordings:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/stay-certified-sap-s-4hana-asset-management?searchId=ad82a5c6-bcd5-4c46-be3a-7d0bae8f9c3b&listPosition=2

 

Why a system‑based certification?

Because real skills matter.
This new exam format demonstrates not only what you know, but what you can do inside SAP S/4HANA Sales.
A strong signal for customers, partners, and your own professional development.

Ready to boost your skills?

With Learning Journeys, hands‑on systems, and expert Live Sessions, you have everything you need to succeed in the new SAP S/4HANA Sales certifications in 2026.

entry.title: Re: NOT ABLE TO POST NVOICE
entry.content:
Best Practice for Troubleshooting

Check the Account Determination Analysis: Use the Account Determination Analysis tool in billing to see which field combination is failing. For that combination, you should have configured vkoa

Verify Master Data: Ensure customer and material master data are complete (account assignment group, reconciliation account, valuation class).

Review Customizing: Confirm that all combinations of chart of accounts, account key, and assignment groups are maintained.

Test with Another Customer/Material: Helps isolate whether the issue is master data or configuration.

In short, besides missing account assignment groups, the error can stem from incomplete customizing, missing G/L accounts, incorrect pricing procedure setup, or gaps in customer/material master data.

entry.title: Re: Discovery Package: Test, Demo, and Development for SAP Cloud ERP Private
entry.content:
@perpetuum_mobile 

hi,

The limited duration of the system is a side effect of the SLAs we introduced to keep the overall price as low as possible. This includes a defined set of self‑service requests (which will be extended over time) as well as security patching within planned maintenance windows. However, the system will not receive upgrades and will naturally become outdated after approximately 24 months. We understand that this may create challenges, and we are actively exploring options to support a smooth transition from one system instance to the next.
In addition, we will soon introduce another TDD offering for Cloud Private, with the same scope and service levels that customers receive in their productive environments. As a result, the system duration will not be limited. This offering will differ in several ways: the system will not be preconfigured, and the pricing model will reflect the extended SLA coverage. Planned availability is currently targeted for late Q2.

best regards, Bettina

entry.title: Re: Service Order Template Migration – Missing Fields (Pr...
entry.content:
Hello @Simon_Lehnert 

Thank you for your question.

Question: Is there a supported way to populate Product ID and Template Search Term during migration?

Answer: The standard migration object for Service Order Templates does not provide those fields, so they cannot be loaded via the migration template. A Public Cloud compliant approach is to migrate the base template first and then maintain additional attributes in post load step. See Communication Scenarios for Service for more details.

Question: Are these fields technically supported for update via API?

Answer: Please verify that via .../API_SERVICE_ORDER_TEMPLATE_SRV/$metadata whether Product ID and Template Search Term exist as properties on A_ServiceOderTemplate. If they are not in metadata, they are not supported for update via the API.

Question: Under which conditions would API_SERVICE_ORDER_TEMPLATE_SRV return something

Answer: In Public Cloud, retrieving data via API depends on proper setup of Communication Scenarios and Communication Arrangements and th authorisations of the technical communication user. If the service is reachable but returns an empty list, the most common causes are missing or incorrect communication arrangement setup or authorisation or your filtering settings within the organisation.

To address the potential reason why API_SERVICE_ORDER_TEMPLATE_SRV return empty result, “empty but 200 OK” typically happens in public cloud if:

1. You are not calling it via a proper Communication Arrangement User. Even if the endpoint is reachable, retrieving data depends on the authorisation of the calling user. You can fix it by ensuring:

  • You use a Communication Arrangement that exposes the service
  • You use the communication user generated in that same arrangement
  • Call the URL exactly as the arrangement has provided

2. Another reason could be implicit authorisation filtering on your organisational data. Service template often carries organisation restrictions (e.g. sales org). If the API user has no authorisation or no access to the org objects, you can get an empty list. You can solve this by:

  • Testing with a template that has minimal org restrictions or create a test template
  • Ensuring the communication user is assigned the correct authorisation via the arrangement.

3. You are getting the correct service root but not the correct entity set or format. Consider adding “&format=json”. Set headers to accept json.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: New System Based Certifications for SAP S/4HANA Sales – Now Available!
entry.content:
These updated exams consist of real, hands‑on tasks executed directly in an SAP system. The result: a much stronger focus on applied skills and real‑world capability. In the area of Sales, we offer certifications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition – Sales and for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition – Sales.

How to Prepare Successfully

1. Explore the official Learning Journeys (free on learning.sap.com)

Public Edition – Sales
👉 https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition-sales
Private Edition – Sales
👉 https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sales-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition

These structured learning paths guide you through all essential topics included in the certification.

2. Get hands‑on practice

Use the available hands‑on training systems to run through exercises, demos, and end‑to‑end scenarios.
This practical experience is key for the new system‑based exam format. Hands-on systems can be found in the Learning Journeys.

3. Join the “Get Certified!” Live Sessions

SAP experts provide guidance, highlight important focus areas, and answer your questions — perfect for a confident exam day. Join the sessions or watch the recordings:

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-implementation-consultant-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sales?searchId=63470ea3-be21-4301-97dc-1f476feecf10&listPosition=3

https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-associate-sap-s-4hana-cloud-private-edition-sales

Why a system‑based certification?

Because real skills matter.
This new exam format demonstrates not only what you know, but what you can do inside SAP S/4HANA Sales.
A strong signal for customers, partners, and your own professional development.

Ready to boost your skills?

With Learning Journeys, hands‑on systems, and expert Live Sessions, you have everything you need to succeed in the new SAP S/4HANA Sales certifications in 2026.

entry.title: SAP Core Logistics for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition, 2025 FPS00 & FPS01 – What’s New
entry.content:
We are proud to introduce the first-ever “What’s New” release blog for Core Logistics in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, highlighting the 2025 FPS00 and FPS01 releases. This update has been a long time coming, and it reflects sustained commitment across our teams to bring durable, enterprise-grade capabilities to our customers. Despite significant challenges over the past years, our dedicated engineering teams have persisted in delivering a set of high-quality features and enhancements.

Core Logistics provides the foundational execution and planning capabilities that underpin end-to-end logistics processes in SAP S/4HANA. Within the broader SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain portfolio, Core Logistics sits at the center — providing the essential, reliable core that enables advanced planning, warehouse management, transportation management and industry-specific extensions to work together seamlessly. The 2025 release enhances that foundation with targeted improvements designed to increase operational resilience, simplify integration, and improve day-to-day usability for logistics professionals.

In the following sections, we’ll highlight the key innovations in this release, show how they deliver real-world value to customers, and provide resources to support implementation and adoption. We’re excited to share this milestone and grateful to the teams and customers who helped make it possible.

As I missed publishing this piece alongside the initial SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 release, and with the recent release of FPS01, I shall combine both releases into one single blog. And we shall begin this overview with the highlights delivered in FPS00.

One more important thing to note is that I shall group the delivered innovations according to their corresponding solution capability. For those new to Logistics Execution – or as a quick refresher - here are the key capability categories we’ll be exploring:

  • Delivery Management – a component of SAP Logistics Execution, Delivery Management is an important part of the logistics chain in which guaranteed customer service and distribution planning support play major roles. In Delivery Management, all delivery procedure decisions can be made at the start of the process by: (1) Considering general business agreements with your customer; (2) Recording special material requests; (3) Defining shipping conditions in the sales order. The result is an efficient and largely automatic shipping process in which manual changes are only necessary under certain circumstances.
  • Handling Unit Management – used to map packing-controlled logistics in the SAP system, HU Management does not consider the movements of the individual materials, instead it maps the movements of the handling units that contain these materials. Available in all core logistic areas where logistic material bookings can take place, this feature uses handling units to map logistical processes to simplify the processing of goods movements thus optimizing logistical processes. This simplified process includes the existing packing function across core logistics and the storage unit processing in the Warehouse Management system.
  • Inventory Management - Inventory Management is part of the Materials Management module and is fully integrated into the entire logistics system. Material is procured from external or internal sources based on the requirements determined by Material Requirements Planning. The delivery is entered in Inventory Management as a goods receipt. The material is stored (and managed under Inventory Management) until it is delivered to customers (Sales & Distribution), or is used for internal purposes (for example, for production).

CORE LOGISTICS IN SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00

Delivery Management

Supply to Customer (S2C) Integration

The first highlight I want to mention is one tightly linked to EWM. This is a foundational update which will allow users to send deliveries that are relevant for the Supply to Customer (S2C) scenario to EWM. Just-in-Time (JIT) processing is a common practice in manufacturing industries for efficient demand-driven production and logistics across supply chains. Just-In-Time Supply to Customer covers JIT processes from the perspective of a supplier. This update for Delivery Management enables production warehouse operators to work with the supply-to-customer (S2C) process for Just-In-Sequence (JIS) calls when using Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) embedded in SAP S/4HANA in their storage locations.

After the production and packing of the sequenced component groups, the system creates outbound deliveries using the Managed Customer JIT Calls by Component Groups app, including handling unit (HU) numbers from (multi-level) packing. These deliveries are then transferred to EWM. PLEASE NOTE that on the EWM side this feature will be enabled as of the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release from February 2026.

Customizing updates in Delivery Management

In Customizing activity Activate Decentralized Warehouse System, users can choose how SAP S/4HANA communicates with the external Warehouse Management system. In addition, customizing activity of Activate Service-Based Communication has been removed.

Furthermore, Activate Decentralized Warehouse System has been extended with the table column Comm. External WM. In this new column, users can select SOAP Web Services to enable the external warehouse management services. This configuration was previously made in Customizing activity Activate Service-Based Communication, which has been removed to avoid redundancy.

Inventory Management

OData API: Material Document - Read, Create

The OData API Material Document - Read, Create (API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT) has been enhanced with the following new fields:

  • ReservationItemRecordType - In the case of a discontinued material in a reservation from a production order, the value provided here indicates whether the discontinued material or the follow-up material should be consumed (“[   ]” = The discontinued material should be consumed; “1” = The follow-up material should be consumed)
  • BatchbySupplier - The Supplier Batch field has been added. This is the number used to manage the batch on the supplier’s side.

SOAP API: Material Document – Create

The SOAP API Material Document - Create (MaterialDocumentCreateRequest_Async_In) has been enhanced with a new field ReservationItemRecordType. For reservations coming from production orders, it is now possible to indicate whether to use a discontinued material or its follow-up material when the goods movement are created (“[   ]” = The discontinued material should be consumed; “1” = The follow-up material should be consumed).

Handling Unit Management

Enabling Auxiliary Packaging Material in 3PL SOAP APIs

Third-party warehouse management APIs enable data exchange with warehouse management systems in external systems. The main use of these APIs is to communicate with third-party warehouse systems and logistics providers. These services can be used to send and receive goods from the external warehouse. In addition, it's possible to update and split outgoing deliveries.

With the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS00 release, the 3rd Party Logistics SOAP APIs has been enhanced to capture secondary packaging information in the field AdditionalPackaging within the Handling unit node. The following SOAP APIs have been updated to support this enhancement:

  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_SHIP_ORDER
  • WAREHOUSEINTERMEDIATEUPDATE_IN
  • WAREHOUSESTOCKTRANSFERRECEIPTA
  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_STOCK_TRANS
  • WAREHOUSESHIPPINGADVICE_IN

Both Customizing activities can be accessed in transaction SPRO under Logistics Execution à Integration of External Warehouse Systems.

Enabling Key User Extensibility at the Handling Unit Header in 3PL SOAP APIs

Another enhancement for 3rd Party Logistics APIs is that key user extensibility was enabled at the Handling Unit header in the 3PL SOAP APIs used for external warehouse management. The following SOAP APIs were enabled to support custom fields creation at Handling Unit header via the Key User Extensibility app:

  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_SHIP_ORDER
  • WAREHOUSEINTERMEDIATEUPDATE_IN
  • WAREHOUSESTOCKTRANSFERRECEIPTA
  • CO_LEIDW_WAREHOUSE_STOCK_TRANS
  • WAREHOUSESHIPPINGADVICE_IN

To use these services, they must first be configured in the S/4HANA system. For this, SAP provides a services group and AIF scenario. For more information about configuring the services in the SOA system, see the related sources for External Warehouse Management Services and for Central Configuration.

Enhancement of Handling Unit API with ISO Codes for Units of Measurement

 A final highlight worth mentioning is the enhancement of the Handling Unit (HU) API, which now includes new fields to support ISO-compliant units of measurement.

 At the Handling Unit header level, the following fields have been enabled to support ISO codes for units of measurement.

:

Field name 

Description 

HndlgUnitTareVolumeUnitISOCode

Tare volume unit of measure of a handling unit in ISO code.

HndlgUnitTareVolumeUnitSAPCode

Tare volume unit of measure of a handling unit in SAP code.

HndlgUnitTareWeightUnitISOCode

Tare weight unit of measure of a handling unit in ISO code.

HndlgUnitTareWeightUnitSAPCode

Tare weight unit of measure of a handling unit in SAP code.

UnitOfMeasureDimensionISOCode

Dimension unit of measure in ISO code.

UnitOfMeasureDimensionSAPCode

Dimension unit of measure in SAP code.

VolumeUnitISOCode

Volume unit of measure ISO code.

VolumeUnitSAPCode

Volume unit of measure in SAP code.

WeightUnitISOCode

Weight unit of measure ISO code.

WeightUnitSAPCode

Weight unit of measure of in SAP code.

 

 At the Handling Unit item level, the following fields have been enabled to support ISO codes for units of measurement

:

Field name 

Description 

HndlgUnitQuantityUnitISOCode

Quantity unit of a handling unit in ISO Code.

HndlgUnitQuantityUnitSAPCode

Quantity unit of a handling unit in SAP Code.

 

CORE LOGISTICS IN SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01

Delivery Management

Persistence of Asset Postings in Delivery Structure

With this feature, we have smoothed the process of managing assets across multiple decentralized warehouse locations. Previously, when assets were moved between decentralized warehouses, the asset reference information would sometimes get lost in translation during the delivery process. Consequently, without clear asset identification, the system would throw posting errors, forcing users to manually trace which specific company asset was being moved.

With the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release, asset references can be embedded directly into delivery documents from the get-go. Now, when an asset moves through the warehouse network, its unique identifier travels with it every step of the way, from the initial goods receipt to the final goods issue posting. For logistics managers, this translates to fewer system interruptions, reduced manual intervention, and most importantly, reliable asset tracking across your entire warehouse ecosystem.

To enable this feature, the delivery communication structure KOMDLGN is extended with the asset fields ANLN1 (main asset number) and ANLN2 (asset subnumber). These fields are populated from the goods movement data (MSEG) during delivery generation and persist in the delivery item data (LIPS). During post goods issue, the same fields are mapped back from the delivery to the goods movement interface to ensure that the asset information is available for final posting.

Enhanced Transfer of Control Dates to support Multistage Intercompany Sales and Stock Transfer Processes

The Transfer of Control (ToC) refers to the specific point in time when ownership or responsibility for goods officially shifts from one party to another. This is critical for revenue recognition and accounting purposes, as it determines when a sale can be recorded and when inventory responsibility changes hands.

When dealing with multistage intercompany sales or stock transfers, such as when goods pass through multiple legal entities before reaching their destination, tracking exactly when control transfers at each handoff point becomes exponentially more complicated.

To support multistage intercompany sales and stock transfer process, the Transfer of Control (ToC) Dates in the outbound delivery header has been enhanced by introducing four new internal ToC-Date groups directly in the header. These additional fields provide the granularity needed to precisely coordinate and document each transfer of control event as goods move between the various organizations involved in your multistage process. Whether you’re managing a three-way intercompany sale or routing stock through regional distribution entities, you now have the tools to accurately capture each ownership transition along the journey.

For more information on Multistage Intercompany Sales process and the Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer process, please read the following Help Portal documentation:

  • Multistage Intercompany Sales
  • Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer

Other Delivery Management feature enrichments

And lastly with regards to Delivery Management enhancements delivered with the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 release, I would like to briefly mention the following extensibility updates:

  • RAP Object updates – two RAP Business Objects (BO) available in Delivery Management have been updated. For the RAP BO I_INBOUNDDELIVERYTP we enabled editing of the Putaway Quantity field, as well as enabled the use of the PostPartialGoodsReceipt action for inbound deliveries. For the I_OUTBOUNDDELIVERYTP RAP BO, the Picking Quantity field is now editable. Furthermore, RAP events are now called when a delivery is assigned to or removed from a shipment using transaction Create Shipment (VT01N) or Change Shipment (VT02N). This allows you to effectively use the BO with shipments.
  • Extensibility for Goods Movements – with this release we also allow users now to extend and change data in a delivery before goods movement is posted. For this, the Business Add-In Goods Movement Data Modification (LE_SHP_SPE_GM_CREATE) can be used. The BAdI can be found in the Customizing (SPRO) for Logistics Execution (LE) under Extended Warehouse Management Integration > Transfer and Inventory Management > Business Add-Ins (BAdIs).

For more information on SAP’s Clean Core strategy, and how our extensibility updates factor into it, please read the Clean Core extensibility white paper.

Inventory Management

Prevention of Goods Receipt Posting for Purchase Order Items with 'Delivery Completed' Indicator Set

This feature introduces a safeguard that prevents goods receipts from being posted against purchase order items already marked with the 'Delivery Completed' indicator (field ELIKZ). When an attempt is made to post a goods receipt for such items, the system displays a customizable message. If the message category is configured as 'Error Message', the system will halt the goods receipt posting entirely for any items where the 'Delivery Completed' indicator is set.

This enhancement is particularly valuable for organizations looking to maintain tighter control over their inventory records. It eliminates the risk of inadvertently posting additional receipts against purchase order lines that have already been closed out – a scenario that can lead to inventory discrepancies and reconciliation headaches down the line.

On the configuration side, administrators have full control over how the system responds to these posting attempts. A new message (message class: M7, message number: 506) has been introduced in customizing table T160M specifically for this purpose. The message category options include 'No Message', 'Information', 'Warning', and 'Error Message', with 'No Message' set as the default. For organisations wanting to enforce strict compliance and prevent any goods receipts against completed items, setting the message category to 'Error Message' will ensure the system blocks these postings outright.

Support for Multistage Intercompany Sales and Stock Transfer Processes in Inventory Management

Inventory Management (MM-IM) receives a significant capability boost with this release, now fully supporting goods issue and goods receipt processes within multistage intercompany sales and stock transfer scenarios.

The Customizing activity 'Assign Movement Types to Value Chain Process Steps' has been expanded to accommodate these multistage scenarios. Dedicated movement types are now available specifically for multistage intercompany sales and stock transfers, and administrators have the flexibility to customize these movement types to align with their organization’s specific operational requirements.

Furthermore, the system now handles timing mismatches in the transfer process. In real-world operations, goods don’t always move in a perfectly sequential manner – sometimes an inbound delivery gets confirmed at the receiving plant before all intermediate steps in the chain have been completed. Previously, this could cause system errors and processing bottlenecks. Now, the system allows temporary negative Valuated Stock in Transit at the receiving plant when goods transfer occurs ahead of goods receipt posting.

Warehouse Handling Unit field in Material Documents Overview

This release brings improved traceability for handling unit information directly into core inventory documentation. The Material Documents Overview app (App ID: F1077) now includes a dedicated Warehouse Handling Unit field (Technical name: EWM_HUIDENT, Type: /SCWM/DE_HUIDENT), giving users immediate visibility into handling unit details without needing to navigate between multiple screens or systems.

Behind the scenes, several objects have been enhanced to support the persistence of Warehouse Handling Unit Identifiers within Material Documents. This means that when handling unit information flows in from Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the system now retains that data directly in the material document record rather than losing it in translation between systems.

BAPI function modules that have been enhanced with warehouse handling unit information:

  • MAP2I_B2017_GM_ITEM_TO_IMSEG
  • MP2E_IMSEG_TO_2017_ITEM_CREATE

The IDoc Segment E1BP2017_GM_ITEM_CREATE1 has also been enhanced.

Furthermore, two Core Data Services (CDS) views in Inventory have been updated to include the Warehouse HU field, namely the Material Document Item view (I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2) and the Goods Movement view (I_GOODSMOVEMENTDOCUMENT).

The Warehouse Handling Unit field is populated in the material document only when it is explicitly passed through the BAPI, IDoc, or OData interfaces.

Other Core Logistics Updates

And since the Core Data Services view for Material Document Item has already been mentioned, I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2 has been enhanced with service document fields, namely:

  • ServiceDocument
  • ServiceDocumentItem
  • ServiceDocumentType

Lastly, I want to go over two Extensibility updates that were delivered with this release:

  • The OData API ‘Material Document - Read, Create’ (API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT) has been enhanced with Warehouse Handling Unit field. More specifically, the ‘Document Items’ entity contains a new property, ‘HandlingUnitExternalID’.
  • Key user extensibility has been enabled for the Packing Instruction Header through SAP GUI and Odata API. Users can create custom fields for packing instruction table PACKKP using business context PACKINGINSTRUCTION via Key user extensibility. The created fields can be managed via SAP GUI transactions POP1/POP2/POP3, as well as via the via the OData API API_PACKINGINSTRUCTION.

Artificial Intelligence: Using Joule in batch management

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 2025 FPS01 release, batch management gets a modern, conversational upgrade with this release through Joule integration. Users can now query batch master information using natural language, making navigation easier and more intuitive. Simply ask Joule to search the batch required for your need, using the batch master attributes, Joule fetches the batch master information based on the criteria provided in the prompt.

The natural language queries support several key fields, including batch number, material, plants, batch status, manufacturing date, shelf-life expiry date, and supplier. This makes it easier to pull up batch information whenever needed, such as to check expiry dates for quality purposes or verify supplier details for traceability requirements.

When deeper analysis or updates are required, Joule provides seamless navigation directly to the batch master object page within the Manage Batches app. From there, users can explore additional batch details, modify information as needed, or take further actions. This integration represents a step forward in making everyday inventory tasks more accessible and efficient through AI-assisted interaction.

FIND OUT MORE!

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information on Multistage Intercompany Sales process and the Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer process, please read the following Help Portal documentation:

  • Multistage Intercompany Sales
  • Multistage Intercompany Stock Transfer

For more information on SAP’s Clean Core strategy please read the Clean Core extensibility white paper.

entry.content:
Hi All,

I would like to ask for support regarding fixed asset migration into SAP S4HANA cloud public edition via migration cockpit for a French company. 

We are migrating an asset which was acquired in February 2020, depreciation start date is 01/02/2020. 

Useful life 4 years:

 

Just to mention, we have 2 ledgers (0L as operational ledger and 2L as local ledger).

The master data was properly migrated. However we are having issues with the second part of migration - the fixed asset postings. In template for fixed asset-postings we have the posting key tab fulfilled as follows:

And the cumulative values tab:

With this file being simulated, we are receiving an error messages for 2L ledger (0L is successfully simulated):

-depreciation area 93 does not manage APC values

I do not understand, how could the acquisition value become negative, if the acquisition cost is 848,00 and depreciation 847,99. There should be 1 cent left. 

This is the fixed asset in system:

Legacy data transfer settings:

And migration key date is 31.12.2025

The 92 and 93 depreciation areas are specific for companies operating in FR. Is there something wrong with the set-up? What should we change to be able to migrate the asset? 

Is there anyone who has experience with FR fixed asset migration?

Thanks in advance

Zuzana Gersiova

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to set a monthly frequency (1x) for a...
entry.content:
I've found the answer that could be implemented here so just wanted to update this here. 

In Signavio, the weekly pattern options can be misleading for monthly processes. The simulation engine actually relies on an annualized frequency, so the correct way to model a task that runs once per month is simply to set the start event’s frequency to 12 cases per year. Signavio documentation confirms that simulation requires specifying the frequency per year for start events.

If your version forces weekly input, you can approximate this by selecting “full week” and entering 0.23 cases per week (12 ÷ 52).

However, using the annual frequency is the intended and more accurate method.

Hope this helps 🙂

Stay tuned and join on You Tube

See how SAP is transforming Ariba into an AI‑native source‑to‑pay platform

SAP is reimagining Ariba as an AI‑native, modern source‑to‑pay platform designed to simplify daily work, improve decision-making, and streamline procurement-to-pay processes. Read the blog to see practical examples, product highlights, and what these changes mean for faster cycle times, stronger cost control, and lower risk across your organization.

Read the full blog

New SAP for Me Services & Support dashboard-now live

We’ve launched a redesigned, user‑centric Services & Support overview tab to make support faster and easier. Enjoy a prominent search, actionable cards for Manage Cases, Quick Access (CIC), service requests and recommendations, unified case management, and simplified navigation — all designed to speed resolutions, boost self‑service, and improve outcomes.

Explore the new dashboard and share your feedback

Heads up for the intelligent front door-one digital experience in SAP for Me

SAP’s new unified entry point in SAP for Me will bring conversational and agentic AI to your fingertips. Discover how effortless navigation, advanced self-service, personalized guidance, and lightning-fast task execution will simplify daily workflows and speed decision-making across your SAP landscape. 

Stay tuned for the launch in May. More details: Intelligent Front Door - Info sheet

Introducing CQC Security Optimization for SAP SuccessFactors-Now in pilot!

Protecting the security and availability of your SAP SuccessFactors environment is vital to keeping your business running smoothly. With the CQC Security Optimization service, you gain proactive analysis and insights to help prevent critical issues before they arise.

Learn more

Your starting point for practical SAP enablement

SAP Enterprise Support Academy is now on learning.sap.com, bringing together hands-on learning, expert guidance, practical resources, and expert-led live sessions that sessions that support you, so you can move from planning to action more quickly.

Learn more here

ALM Summit NA - April 14-16 in Newtown Square-March

The landscape of Application Lifecycle Management is transforming, and you can witness this evolution firsthand at the SAP ALM Summit North America 2026.

Dates: April 14–16, 2026
Location: SAP US Headquarters, Newtown Square, PA

Register here

Customer Success Through Trusted Partner Support Expertise

SAP Cloud customers rely on implementation and operations services from SAP’s partner ecosystem, where an effective support collaboration between partner and SAP is essential for Customer Success.  To unlock incremental value, such as faster issue resolution and risk mitigation, customers are encouraged to select and manage partners also based on support quality.

Read the article and learn more

  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy on LSC (learning.sap.com)

entry.title: Re: 2026 Fixed Assets - Not Showing Values on Fixed Assets tab
entry.content: You haven't provided the necessary information: what year did the amortization payments begin in the system? Was it monthly or annual amortization? Did you make an amortization payment in 2025?

entry.title: Re: How to do mass G/L accounts' text addition
entry.content:
Hi, 

You can try FI - G/L account - extend existing record by new org levels template available in "migration cockpit" and use tab "account names".
